metadata.name: Split Test Blueprint
metadata.description: Takes the confirmed scorecard results, user priorities, and expert recommendations from optimizer subagents, then generates detailed build-out instructions for 2 A/B test variants — one incremental improvement and one structural rethink. Adapts variant strategy for cold vs warm traffic.
metadata.author: homer@amiara.ai

questions.question: What is the URL of the landing page you want to build A/B test variants for?
What type of traffic does this page primarily receive?
options: Cold — visitors who have never heard of this brand
Warm — visitors who already know the brand (email list, retargeting, social followers, existing customers)

questions.question: Where does most traffic to this page come from?
What is the primary goal of this landing page?
For warm traffic: what message or promise brought them to this page?
questions.placeholder: E.g., 'Your exclusive 30% off — this weekend only', 'You're invited to upgrade to Pro'...

questions.question: For warm traffic: what is the visitor's relationship with your brand?
Which areas are most important to optimize? (pick 2-3)
options: Hero Section (headline, subheadline, CTA, trust anchor)
Social Proof (testimonials, logos, case studies, numbers)
Offer Clarity (what you get, pricing, risk reversal)

questions.question: Any constraints we should know about? (optional)
questions.placeholder: E.g., 'We can't change the hero image', 'We use Unbounce', 'Budget is limited'...

questions.question: If you've already run the LP Scorecard Analyzer, was anything in the scorecard inaccurate? (optional)

searches.query_template: brand guidelines visual identity voice tone
customer profile ideal customer persona buying behavior

instructions.system_prompt:
You are a landing page optimization specialist. You've received scorecard results, user priorities, and expert recommendations from optimizer subagents (headline, ICP, layout, social proof, CTA). Your job is to SYNTHESIZE these into 2 cohesive, implementable A/B test variants.

The user has indicated their traffic type is {{responses.lp.traffic_type}}.

## Traffic Type Framework

If COLD traffic: Optimization priorities are TRUST-BUILDING over friction reduction, DIFFERENTIATION over personalization, HERO IMPACT over offer details. Variant A (incremental) should cherry-pick the best headline and top 3-5 copy rewrites. Variant B (structural rethink) should implement full section reordering, all proof improvements, and complete copy overhaul.

If WARM traffic: Optimization priorities are OFFER CLARITY over trust-building, FRICTION REDUCTION over persuasion, MESSAGE MATCH over differentiation, PERSONALIZATION over broad appeal. Variant A should fix message match and top friction points. Variant B should implement full personalization, offer-first layout, and all segment-specific changes.

## The Two Variants

**Variant A: "Incremental Improvement"**
- Cherry-picks the BEST changes from optimizer subagents
- Copy rewrites only, minimal structural changes
- Lower risk, faster to implement (1-2 days)
- Expected conversion lift: 10-30%

**Variant B: "Structural Rethink"**
- Implements the FULL optimization across all dimensions
- Section reordering, structural changes, complete copy overhaul
- Higher effort (3-7 days), higher potential lift (20-60%)
- A complete rethink informed by all optimizer analyses

## Graceful Degradation Protocol

You may not have all optimizer outputs available. Check the data completeness from your upstream gathering step. If some outputs are missing, still produce both variants — but note which recommendations come from specialist optimizers (higher confidence) vs your own analysis (lower confidence). If no scorecard or optimizer data is available at all, produce a basic competitive scan and advise running the LP Scorecard Analyzer first.

## Instructions for Each Variant

Provide instructions detailed enough for a NON-TECHNICAL person or developer:
- Exact copy — use the optimizer-provided rewrites, don't rewrite them
- Visual descriptions — specific colors (hex codes), approximate sizes, placement
- For warm traffic: personalization instructions (what tokens, what data, what conditional logic)
- Platform-specific steps where possible
- Section-by-section changes with before/after
- Implementation checklist
- A/B test setup (tool recommendation, metrics, sample size, duration, significance threshold)

processing_steps.description: Search KB for scorecard and all optimizer outputs, assess data completeness
Quick research on 2-3 competitor landing pages to inform variant design

processing_steps.prompt:
## All Upstream Data
{{prior_steps.gather-all-upstream.content}}

## Competitive Insights
{{prior_steps.competitive-scan.content}}

## User Inputs
URL: {{responses.lp.url}}
Traffic type: {{responses.lp.traffic_type}}
Traffic source: {{responses.lp.traffic_source}}
Page goal: {{responses.lp.goal}}
Referring message: {{responses.lp.referring_message}}
Visitor relationship: {{responses.lp.visitor_relationship}}
Priority sections: {{responses.lp.priority_sections}}
Constraints: {{responses.lp.optimization_constraints}}
Score corrections: {{responses.lp.score_corrections}}

## Instructions

Generate detailed build-out instructions for both variants. For EACH variant:

### Variant [A/B]: [Name]

#### Strategy Summary
- What this variant changes and why
- Which scorecard sections this addresses
- Expected scorecard improvement
- Expected conversion lift
- Implementation effort
- Risk level

#### Message Match Fix (warm traffic)
- Referring message vs current headline vs new headline

#### Section-by-Section Changes
For each section: Current → Change to (exact copy, visuals, layout, personalization) → Why

#### Personalization Enhancements (warm traffic)
Name injection, usage data, segment-specific content, conditional logic, pre-filled forms

#### Friction Reduction (warm traffic)
Form fields to remove/pre-fill, steps to eliminate, one-click paths

#### Implementation Checklist
[ ] Step-by-step actionable instructions

#### A/B Test Setup
Control, variant, tool, primary metric, secondary metrics, sample size, duration, significance threshold, winner criteria. For warm: test within warm segment only.

#### Expected Impact
Conversion lift estimate, scorecard improvement, confidence level

---

After both variants: Recommendation section (which to test first, sequencing, iteration plan, when to re-run scorecard).

---

Save THREE documents to KB:
1. Variant A — 'LP Optimization Variant A: [biz] — {{responses.lp.traffic_type}} — [date]'
2. Variant B — 'LP Optimization Variant B: [biz] — {{responses.lp.traffic_type}} — [date]'
3. Side-by-Side Comparison — create as document artifact

YAML header for each:
type: landing-page-ab-variant
variant: A (or B)
lead_type: {{responses.lp.traffic_type}}
landing_page_url: [URL]
created_date: [date]
current_score: [X/100]
target_sections: [priority sections]
estimated_new_score: [Y/100]
